chunks.text: The tables below contain enrollment guidelines for Electrical Engineering classes. You can also find detailed, class-specific enrollment information at classes.berkeley.edu. CLASS NOTES sections can provide important information and details for classes that have specific enrollment requirements/policy, along with instructor policy. Please review them prior to enrolling or adding to the waitlist. To see semester-specific enrollment information and FAQs, sign up for FREE to the EECS 101 section of the EDStem Discussion Dashboard. For more information on getting into CS classes, see our Getting into CS Classes webpage. If you have questions regarding getting into EECS/EE classes, please email: ee-scheduling@eecs.berkeley.edu.

For EECS 16A, enrollment guidelines vary by student type. All undergraduate UC Berkeley students have reserved seats in Phase 1, with waitlisting and enrollment based on availability in later phases. Graduate UC Berkeley students can waitlist and enroll in remaining seats. All MEng students cannot enroll or waitlist in undergraduate classes. Concurrent enrollment applications are processed starting week 2. Visiting and exchange students cannot enroll during the academic year.

For EECS 16B, enrollment guidelines are similar to EECS 16A. All undergraduate UC Berkeley students have reserved seats in Phase 1, with waitlisting and enrollment based on availability in later phases. Graduate UC Berkeley students can waitlist and enroll in remaining seats. All MEng students cannot enroll or waitlist in undergraduate classes. Concurrent enrollment applications are processed starting week 2. Visiting and exchange students cannot enroll during the academic year.

For upper division EE/EECS classes, declared EECS, joint, ECE, and CS majors have no restrictions in Phase 1. Non-EECS COE students can enroll in reserve seats or waitlist, with enrollment after reserve caps end. All non-COE undergraduate students can waitlist most classes, with enrollment after reserve caps end. Graduate students can waitlist and enroll after reserve caps end. Concurrent enrollment applications are processed starting week 2. Visiting and exchange students cannot enroll during the academic year.

For graduate level classes, EECS graduate students and MEng students have no restrictions. Non-EECS graduate students can enroll and waitlist most classes, with automatic enrollment after reserve caps. Non-EECS MEng students are prevented from waitlisting or enrolling. UC Berkeley undergraduates are blocked from enrolling in grad classes unless they have permission codes. Concurrent enrollment applications are processed starting week 2. Visiting and exchange students cannot enroll during the academic year.

The default policy for EECS/EE classes is no time conflicts with lectures or labs. Some classes may allow time conflicts based on instructor preferences, but alternate exams are not offered unless confirmed. Students must ensure they can take both final exams and should not enroll if there is a conflict. It is the student's responsibility to confirm exam arrangements.

Prerequisites for EECS/EE classes are generally strongly recommended and not formally enforced, as stated on the EE courses page. Some classes have formally enforced prerequisites, requiring completion with a passing grade. Others have informally enforced prerequisites, where students may be dropped if not met. Co-requisites may lead to drops at the instructor's request. For issues, contact ee-scheduling@eecs.berkeley.edu.
